subject: Geology
title: Tectonic vs. Non-Tectonic Quakes
description: A quick win: understand Tectonic vs. Non-Tectonic Quakes.

hook.text: Not all earthquakes come from plate tectonics. Some are caused by volcanic eruptions, collapsing caves, or humans.

buildup.text: Tectonic earthquakes result from plate movement. Non-tectonic quakes have volcanic, collapse, or human causes.

discovery.text: Volcanic earthquakes signal magma movement. Collapse quakes happen when underground caverns give way.

twist.text: Induced seismicity from fracking, mining, and reservoir filling is a growing concern in non-earthquake zones.

climax.text: Understanding earthquake types helps separate natural risks from human-caused ones and guides proper response.

punchline.text: Not every quake is nature's fault.

quiz.question: What is induced seismicity?
options: Earthquakes caused by human activities like fracking
